paper_title: Does Birthplace Diversity Affect Economic Complexity? Cross-country Evidence
research_answer: Yes, and the data provide a way to quantify how much. When economic complexity is added as a control in regressions linking birthplace diversity to GDP per capita, the partial correlation between diversity and income drops by about 20%. This means ECI acts as a meaningful — though partial — channel through which immigrant diversity translates into higher income. In other words, one reason diverse immigrant populations raise incomes is that they help countries export a broader and more sophisticated range of products, which is exactly what economic complexity captures.
supporting_quotes: Note that when ECI is controlled for there is a significant reduction of the partial correlation between diversity and GDP per capita, of around 20%. This suggests that ECI is, in part, one of the channels through which birthplace diversity affects income.

data_points.metric: Share of diversity-to-income effect mediated by ECI
